LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JOSEPH ENGLERT, EXPORT ASSIST, INC., EXPORT ASSIST INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF PLEADS SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE CLAIM, PARAGRAPH 23 DOES NOT SPECIFY WHEN THE CAUSE OF ACTION ACCRUED AND PARAGRAPHS 36-39 SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE THE DISCOVERY RULE. THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT SHE WAS AN INVESTOR AT PARAGRAPHS 21 AND 29 AND THAT RELATIONSHIP CAN CREATE A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP. SEE CLEVELAND V. JOHNSON (2012) 209 CAL.APP.4TH 1315, 1339. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION. CIVIL CODE 1714.01 DOES NOT CREATE A DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND THE TORT IS NEGLIGENCE. SEE BURGESS V. SUP. CRT. (1992) 2 CAL.4TH 1064, 1072. THE DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES A BREACH OF DUTY. THE DEMURRER TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION IS OVERRULED. PARAGRAPHS 91 AND 92 SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE WRONGFUL DOMINION BECAUSE DEFENDANTS FAILED TO RETURN PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

All of the causes of action are well-pleaded. The Court denies the request for judicial notice. Even if the Court considered those documents, they do not resolve whether the amended cross-complaint states sufficient facts to support the causes of action. Order signed in open court. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-23-2013 Continued To Sep-18-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. rULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANTs JOSEPH ENGLERT, EXPORT ASSIST, INC., EXPORT ASSIST INTERNATIONAL, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the first cause of action for intentional misrepresentation cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support the claim, paragraph 23 does not specify when the cause of action accrued and paragraphs 36-39 sufficiently allege the discovery rule. The demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that she was an investor at paragraphs 21 and 29 and that relationship can create a fiduciary relationship. See Cleveland v. Johnson (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 1315, 1339. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 4th cause of action. Civil Code 1714.01 does not create a distinct cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress and the tort is negligence. See Burgess v. Sup. Crt. (1992) 2 Cal.4th 1064, 1072. The demurrer to the 5th cause of action for negligence is overruled. Plaintiff alleges a breach of duty. The demurrer to the 6th cause of action for conversion is overruled. Paragraphs 91 and 92 sufficiently allege wrongful dominion because defendants failed to return plaintiff's property. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
